name: Mirror contacts across systems with HubSpot and Airtable and log the results

parameters.text: =✅ Mirror contacts across systems with HubSpot and Airtable and log the r — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 6 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Pull Side A (HubSpot)

### Pull Side A (HubSpot)

Looks up an existing HubSpot contact by last-modified date — finds records the pipeline hasn't processed yet.

Ports: ①→ Diff the Two Systems · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Pull Side B (Airtable)

Searches Airtable's 'Contacts' table by formula (`={LastModified} > DATEADD(NOW(), -1, 'days')`) for records the pipeline hasn't handled yet.

Ports: ①→ Diff the Two Systems · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Diff the Two Systems

Diffs two streams by key into 4 lanes: only-in-A, same, different, only-in-B — the heart of the sync.

Ports: ①→ Create Missing in Airtable · ②→ Count In-Sync Rows · ③→ Apply Updates to Airtable · ④→ Backfill into HubSpot

### Create Missing in Airtable

Creates a new record in Airtable's 'Contacts' table.

Ports: ①→ Gather Sync Results · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Count In-Sync Rows

Collapses all items into a single summary item (for counts and run stats).

Next: Gather Sync Results

### Apply Updates to Airtable

Upserts into Airtable's 'Contacts' table, matched on `contactId` — safe to re-run without creating duplicates.

Ports: ①→ Gather Sync Results · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Backfill into HubSpot

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Ports: ①→ Gather Sync Results · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Gather Sync Results

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Ports: ①→ Fingerprint for Idempotency · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per contact (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Looks up an existing HubSpot contact by last-modified date — finds records the pipeline hasn't processed yet.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (HubSpot)

### Append Audit Row (HubSpot)

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#sales`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Twilio

### Escalate Failure to Twilio

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Appends a new row to 'Contact Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Mirror contacts across systems with HubSpot and Airtable and log the results

**Pattern:** Two-System Sync · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Mirror contacts across systems with HubSpot and Airtable and log the results. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual contact sync is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Pull Side A (HubSpot) → Diff the Two Systems → Create Missing in Airtable → Gather Sync Results → Serialize Run Output (CSV) → Archive Row Snapshot → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to two-system sync without manual effort
- Anyone automating contact sync in sales
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Scales without extra headcount
- Removes copy-paste errors

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Airtable PAT; Google OAuth2 / service account; HubSpot private-app token; Slack OAuth2; Twilio SID + token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Compare Datasets does the matching instead of hand-rolled join logic — one node produces four labeled lanes (only-in-A, matching, different, only-in-B), and each lane gets exactly the action it needs (create / count / update / backfill) instead of one generic 'sync' branch trying to cover every case.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Swap Side A or Side B for a third platform and rewire the merge-by-fields key.
2. Intentionally create a mismatch between the two systems and trace which lane it lands in.
3. Change mergeByFields to a different key and see how the diff results shift.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your contact sync process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
